lesson: Lesson 1: What Part 107 Is and Who Needs It
caption: Decision flowchart distinguishing recreational (44809/TRUST) from commercial (Part 107) operation based on the purpose of the flight.

lesson: Lesson 2: Eligibility and the Path to Certification
caption: Step-by-step pipeline of the Part 107 certification process from study through registration of the drone.

lesson: Lesson 3: The UAG Knowledge Exam
caption: Chart of the five ACS knowledge-area exam weights, emphasizing Operations as the largest area.

lesson: Lesson 4: Staying Current — Recurrent Training
caption: Timeline showing initial certification, then a recurrent online course renewing knowledge recency every 24 calendar months with no in-person retest.

lesson: Lesson 5: Registering the Drone, Remote ID, and the 5 Knowledge Areas Ahead
caption: Three-panel illustration of the Remote ID compliance options: standard RID drone, broadcast module, and operating within a FRIA.

lesson: Lesson 5: Registering the Drone, Remote ID, and the 5 Knowledge Areas Ahead
caption: Roadmap graphic of the five ACS knowledge areas the course covers, with their approximate exam weights.

lesson: Lesson 2: Registration, Marking, and Remote ID
caption: Three Remote ID compliance paths side by side: standard RID drone broadcasting pilot/control-station location, a broadcast module broadcasting take-off location, and a FRIA boundary with a non-RID drone inside.

lesson: Lesson 4: Core Operating Limitations (14 CFR 107.51)
caption: Side-view diagram showing the 400-ft AGL ceiling over open ground and the 400-ft-above-structure exception within a 400-ft radius of a tall tower.

lesson: Lesson 6: Operations Over People & Moving Vehicles, Preflight, and Accident Reporting
caption: Decision flowchart for 14 CFR 107.9: does the event involve serious injury, loss of consciousness, or non-drone property damage over $500? If yes, report within 10 calendar days.

lesson: The National Airspace System: Controlled vs. Uncontrolled
caption: Side-view cross-section of the National Airspace System showing all classes stacked by altitude: Class A above 18,000 MSL, Class B upside-down wedding cake, Class C two-tier, Class D cylinder to surface, Class E floors (surface, 700, 1,200 ft AGL), and Class G below the overlying Class E.

lesson: Controlled Airspace: Classes A, B, C, and D
caption: Sectional excerpt comparing the three towered-airport classes side by side: solid blue Class B concentric rings, solid magenta Class C rings, and a blue dashed Class D ring with a visible boxed ceiling number.

lesson: Controlled Airspace: Classes A, B, C, and D
caption: Three-dimensional cutaway of a Class B 'upside-down wedding cake' showing the surface core and the wider shelves stacked above it, with each shelf's floor/ceiling MSL altitudes labeled.

lesson: Class E, Class G, and Who Needs Authorization
caption: Sectional excerpt showing all three Class E depictions together: dashed-magenta surface-area E around an airport, faded magenta 700 ft AGL vignette fading outward, and faded blue 1,200 ft AGL vignette transition.

lesson: Class E, Class G, and Who Needs Authorization
caption: Example UAS Facility Map (UASFM) grid overlaid on an airport area, showing maximum LAANC altitudes (0, 100, 200, 300, 400 ft AGL) per cell, with low/zero values near the runway and higher values farther out.

lesson: Reading the Sectional Chart: Airports, Airspace Lines & Vignettes
caption: Sectional legend comparison of a blue towered-airport symbol and a magenta non-towered-airport symbol, annotated with fuel-service ticks and the rotating-beacon star.

lesson: Reading the Sectional Chart: Airports, Airspace Lines & Vignettes
caption: A clean reference panel showing each airspace line/vignette style next to its class: solid blue (B), solid magenta (C), blue dashed (D), magenta dashed (E-surface), faded magenta (E 700 AGL), faded blue (E 1,200 AGL).

lesson: Reading the Sectional Chart: Airports, Airspace Lines & Vignettes
caption: Zoomed chart excerpt highlighting airspace floor/ceiling numbers: a Class C shelf labeled 41/12 and a Class D ring with a boxed [25] ceiling, plus an SFC floor callout.

lesson: Airport Data Blocks, Frequencies, Lat/Long & the MEF
caption: Magnified Sectional airport data block with each element labeled: airport name, field elevation MSL, CTAF (bold magenta C in circle), runway length in hundreds of feet, lighting code.

lesson: Airport Data Blocks, Frequencies, Lat/Long & the MEF
caption: Sectional neatline corner showing degree and minute tick marks for latitude and longitude, with an example coordinate read-off annotated.

lesson: Airport Data Blocks, Frequencies, Lat/Long & the MEF
caption: A chart quadrangle bounded by lat/long lines showing a large bold MEF (e.g., 12 with superscript 5 = 12,500 MSL) centered in the block.

lesson: Special Use Airspace, TFRs, NOTAMs & Obstructions
caption: Sectional excerpt with several Special Use Airspace borders (Restricted, Prohibited, MOA, Alert) and the SUA tabulation box listing numbers, altitudes, times of use, and controlling agency.

lesson: Special Use Airspace, TFRs, NOTAMs & Obstructions
caption: Graphical depiction of a TFR ring (e.g., a stadium or VIP TFR) as shown in a flight-planning app, with the prohibited radius and altitude limits labeled.

lesson: Special Use Airspace, TFRs, NOTAMs & Obstructions
caption: Sectional obstruction symbology: single inverted-V tower and a grouped obstruction, each labeled with bold MSL top height and parenthetical AGL height, plus the high-intensity lighted-obstruction symbol.

lesson: Putting It Together: Can I Legally Fly Here?
caption: Decision flowchart for the five-step can-I-fly workflow ending in GO / NO-GO, branching at airspace class and TFR checks.

lesson: Putting It Together: Can I Legally Fly Here?
caption: Mock B4UFLY-style app screen showing a location with its airspace status (e.g., controlled/authorization-required vs. clear), nearby airport, and an active TFR alert.

lesson: The Atmosphere, the Standard Atmosphere & Moisture
caption: Diagram of the ICAO standard atmosphere: a vertical scale showing sea-level temperature 15 degrees C and pressure 29.92 inHg, with the ~2 degrees C per 1,000 ft temperature lapse illustrated up through the troposphere.

lesson: Density Altitude & Its Effect on sUAS Performance
caption: A density-altitude conversion chart: outside air temperature on the horizontal axis, pressure-altitude lines diagonally, density altitude read off the vertical axis - annotated to show that a hot day at a high field yields a very high density altitude.

lesson: Stability, Air Masses & Fronts
caption: A four-panel fronts diagram showing cold, warm, stationary, and occluded fronts: their standard weather-map symbols (blue triangles, red half-circles, alternating symbols, purple combined) plus a side-view cross-section of each showing the air-mass slopes and the resulting cloud and precipitation pattern.

lesson: Clouds, Ceilings, Visibility & the Cloud-Clearance Rules
caption: A cloud-family height chart showing low (stratus, stratocumulus, cumulus), middle (altostratus, altocumulus), and high (cirrus, cirrostratus) clouds plus a towering cumulonimbus, with the FEW/SCT/BKN/OVC sky-cover icons labeled.

lesson: Wind, Thunderstorms, Microbursts & Fog
caption: Cross-section of a microburst: an intense downdraft column striking the ground and spreading outward as a damaging horizontal outflow, with arrows showing the rapidly reversing wind shear an aircraft would encounter passing through it.

lesson: Wind, Thunderstorms, Microbursts & Fog
caption: Three side-by-side cloud cross-sections labeled cumulus, mature, and dissipating, with arrows showing updrafts in the cumulus stage, mixed updrafts/downdrafts and precipitation in the mature stage, and downdrafts in the dissipating stage.

lesson: Reading a METAR & TAF; Weather Sources
caption: An annotated sample METAR and TAF: the METAR 'KSAT 121753Z AUTO 27015G25KT 10SM FEW050 SCT250 27/12 A2992 RMK AO2' with callout labels on each element, and below it a TAF showing the valid-period group and FM/TEMPO/BECMG change groups labeled.

lesson: Reading a METAR & TAF; Weather Sources
caption: A composite of the two official FAA weather websites - aviationweather.gov and 1800wxbrief.com - with the METAR and TAF panels highlighted to show where a Remote PIC pulls observations and forecasts.

lesson: The Forces of Flight on a Small UAS
caption: Quadcopter in forward cruise with the four forces labeled: lift (up), weight (down), thrust (forward), drag (rearward), aircraft tilted into the direction of travel.

lesson: Weight, Balance, and Center of Gravity
caption: Side-by-side CG diagram: balanced multirotor with CG at center vs. one with an off-center payload shifting the CG, showing the aircraft tilting and motors compensating.

lesson: Payload Effects: Flight Time, Maneuverability, and Battery Draw
caption: Simple line chart: flight time (minutes) decreasing as payload weight increases, with a note that wind and high density altitude push the curve lower.

lesson: Density Altitude and Wind: Performance and Endurance
caption: Illustration of 'High, Hot, Humid' raising density altitude: a drone climbing sluggishly on a hot day at high elevation vs. a brisk climb on a cool, low day.

lesson: Battery Care, Hazards, and Pre-Flight Performance Planning
caption: Comparison photo of a flat, healthy LiPo pack vs. a swollen/puffed pack, with a caption noting that swollen packs must be retired.

lesson: Battery Care, Hazards, and Pre-Flight Performance Planning
caption: Sample manufacturer performance table/chart: flight time vs. payload and vs. temperature, illustrating how to read and discount best-case figures.

lesson: Lesson 1: Crew Resource Management & the Remote PIC's Authority
caption: Crew diagram showing RPIC (final authority), person manipulating controls under supervision, and a visual observer, with communication arrows between them.

lesson: Lesson 2: Physiological Factors & Fitness for Flight
caption: IMSAFE checklist graphic listing Illness, Medication, Stress, Alcohol, Fatigue, Emotion with self-check prompts.

lesson: Lesson 3: Aeronautical Decision-Making & the Five Hazardous Attitudes
caption: Table or infographic of the five hazardous attitudes paired with their antidotes (Anti-Authority, Impulsivity, Invulnerability, Macho, Resignation).

lesson: Lesson 4: Risk Management — PAVE & the 3P Model
caption: PAVE checklist infographic: Pilot, Aircraft, enVironment, External pressures, each with example risk items.

lesson: Lesson 5: Radio Communications, Airport Operations & Traffic Patterns
caption: Standard left-hand traffic pattern diagram labeling upwind, crosswind, downwind, base, and final around a runway, with a wind sock indicating wind direction.

lesson: Lesson 5: Radio Communications, Airport Operations & Traffic Patterns
caption: Airport wind indicators: wind sock, tetrahedron, and segmented circle, showing how each indicates wind/landing direction.

lesson: Lesson 6: Maintenance, Preflight Inspection & Recordkeeping
caption: Preflight inspection checklist graphic grouping checks into aircraft, environment, power/links, and crew/documents.

lesson: Lesson 7: Emergency Procedures, Night Operations & Maintaining VLOS
caption: Decision flowchart for in-flight emergencies: lost link, flyaway, GPS loss, low battery, fire — with the correct response for each.

lesson: Lesson 7: Emergency Procedures, Night Operations & Maintaining VLOS
caption: Night drone operation showing anti-collision lighting visible 3 SM and a remote pilot using off-center scanning.

lesson: Lesson 1: How the UAG Exam Works & Test Strategy
caption: Infographic summarizing the UAG exam: 60 questions, 120 minutes, 3 options each, 70% to pass, and the five knowledge-area proportions as a pie/bar chart.

lesson: Lesson 1: How the UAG Exam Works & Test Strategy
caption: Flowchart of the four-step chart-reading workflow (locate point, identify airspace by color, decide authorization, scan for traps).

lesson: Lesson 2: Regulations & Operations Cram Sheet
caption: One-page 'cheat card' graphic listing the high-yield numbers: 400 ft AGL, 87 kt/100 mph, 3 SM, 500/2000 cloud clearance, 0.04 BAC / 8 hr, 107.9 = 10 days/$500.

lesson: Lesson 2: Regulations & Operations Cram Sheet
caption: Table graphic pairing each of the five hazardous attitudes with its antidote.

lesson: Lesson 3: Airspace & Weather Cram Sheet
caption: Side-by-side sectional excerpts: Class B (solid blue), Class C (solid magenta), Class D (dashed blue with boxed ceiling), Class E to surface (dashed magenta), and the 700-ft AGL faded magenta vignette.

lesson: Lesson 3: Airspace & Weather Cram Sheet
caption: An annotated METAR with each group labeled, plus a small sky-cover legend (FEW/SCT/BKN/OVC oktas).

lesson: Lesson 3: Airspace & Weather Cram Sheet
caption: Diagram showing how high, hot, and humid conditions raise density altitude and reduce drone lift/thrust/flight time.

lesson: Lesson 4: Final Readiness Checklist & Next Steps
caption: Flowchart of the post-exam certification process: FTN → pass UAG → Form 8710-13 in IACRA → TSA vetting → temporary certificate → permanent card → register drone.

lesson: Lesson 4: Final Readiness Checklist & Next Steps
caption: Timeline graphic showing the 24-calendar-month recurrent-training cycle and that the certificate itself never expires.
